title: Colvale Fort
tagline: Colvale Fort — heritage city of Goa

overview.short: The Fortaleza de São Sebastião, also called the Tivim Fort, after the village where a part of the long wall structure was once located, is a fort that spans from the village of Tiv
overview.description: The Fortaleza de São Sebastião, also called the Tivim Fort, after the village where a part of the long wall structure was once located, is a fort that spans from the village of Tivim, to the area known as Colvale, in Goa, on the west coast of India. Currently, most of it is in ruins and barely visible. However, parts of the structure are in the process of being restored. It is one in a series of three forts, stretching the length of the Tivim canal – which connects the Moira River to the Chapora River.

topPlaces.name: Colvale
topPlaces.description: Colvale is a census town in North Goa district in the state of Goa, India. It has been a scenic and historic village, though in recent years changed by out-migration and in-migration.

topPlaces.name: Marna, Goa
topPlaces.description: Marna is a village in Bardez taluk or sub-district in North Goa District of Goa State, India.

topPlaces.name: Mapusa
topPlaces.description: Mapusa (Goan Konkani: Mhapxem) is a city in North Goa, India. It is situated 13 km north of the state capital of Panaji.

topPlaces.name: Cunchelim
topPlaces.description: Cunchelim or Kucheli is a village in Bardez taluka, in the district of North Goa, in the state of Goa in India.

topPlaces.name: Moira, Goa
topPlaces.description: Moira ([mɔjɽɛ̃ː]) is a village in the Bardez Taluka of the North Goa District in India. Till the recent past, Moira has been known for its typical, large bananas (known as Mundollchim kellim in Konkani) that grew in the area.

topPlaces.name: St. Jerome Church (Mapusa)
topPlaces.description: St. Jerome Church is a parish of the Catholic Church in Mapusa, Goa, India, in the Archdiocese of Goa and Daman.

topPlaces.name: Saraswat Vidyalaya
topPlaces.description: Saraswat Vidyalaya is an educational institution located in Khorlim, Mapusa, Goa, India. It was founded as a primary school by the Saraswat Education Society in March 1911, and gave instruction in the Marathi language.

topPlaces.name: Konkan
topPlaces.description: The Konkan is a stretch of land by the western coast of India, bound by the river Daman Ganga at Damaon in the north, to Anjediva Island next to Karwar town in the south; with the Arabian Sea to the west and the Deccan Plateau to the east. The hinterland east of the coast has numerous river valleys, riverine islands and the hilly slopes known as the Western Ghats; that lead up into the tablelands of the Deccan.

items.note: Colvale is a census town in North Goa district in the state of Goa, India. It has been a scenic and historic village, though in recent years changed by out-migration and in-migration.
Marna is a village in Bardez taluk or sub-district in North Goa District of Goa State, India.
Mapusa (Goan Konkani: Mhapxem) is a city in North Goa, India. It is situated 13 km north of the state capital of Panaji.
Cunchelim or Kucheli is a village in Bardez taluka, in the district of North Goa, in the state of Goa in India.

Moira ([mɔjɽɛ̃ː]) is a village in the Bardez Taluka of the North Goa District in India. Till the recent past, Moira has been known for its typical, large bananas (known as Mundollchim kellim in Konkani) that grew in the area.
St. Jerome Church is a parish of the Catholic Church in Mapusa, Goa, India, in the Archdiocese of Goa and Daman.

Saraswat Vidyalaya is an educational institution located in Khorlim, Mapusa, Goa, India. It was founded as a primary school by the Saraswat Education Society in March 1911, and gave instruction in the Marathi language.

The Konkan is a stretch of land by the western coast of India, bound by the river Daman Ganga at Damaon in the north, to Anjediva Island next to Karwar town in the south; with the Arabian Sea to the west and the Deccan Plateau to the east. The hinterland east of the coast has numerous river valleys, riverine islands and the hilly slopes known as the Western Ghats; that lead up into the tablelands of the Deccan.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Colvale Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Colvale Fort is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Colvale Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Manohar International Airport, Mopa (North Goa) (13 km) and the nearest railway station is Vasco da Gama (Goa) (25 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Colvale Fort?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Colvale Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Colvale Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Colvale Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Colvale Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Colvale, Marna, Goa, Mapusa, Cunchelim, Moira, Goa.
